mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Lower consumer price index growth reduces inflation expectations and real discount rates, prompting immediate positive equity repricing over a five-day window.
failure_condition: Declining CPIAUCSL values correlate with negative 5-day forward returns or show a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures falling below 400000 million dollars indicates a severe demand shock that suppresses corporate earnings expectations and generates negative returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than half of the periods where RSAFS drops below the 400000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate signals healthy job creation without wage inflation pressure, supporting positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 2.5 percent during a late-cycle expansion phase increases financing costs and triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Elevated FEDFUNDS levels in late-cycle regimes produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed instances.
